review_text: This was the most productive summer I have ever had. Team lunches, demos, and workshops made this feel like a full experience. I never felt like an outsider — the team was genuinely welcoming. I walked away with skills, confidence, and a strong professional network.

I was micromanaged on trivial tasks but given no guidance on actual work. My requests for career guidance were brushed off or postponed indefinitely. Colleagues were dismissive and some were outright rude to interns. I left with no portfolio piece, no reference, and no useful skill.

I felt challenged in the best possible way throughout. I never felt like an outsider — the team was genuinely welcoming. I never felt like an outsider — the team was genuinely welcoming. This internship made my resume stronger and my mindset sharper.

This was the most demotivating professional experience of my life. My final deliverable was never reviewed — it was just archived and ignored. I was repeatedly given contradictory instructions from different supervisors. Future interns deserve far better than what I went through.

Every mentor here is genuinely invested in your growth. I attended client calls and strategy sessions that gave me real exposure. I collaborated with people across five departments, which broadened my view. This is the gold standard of what an internship should look like.

I came in as a student and left feeling like a professional. The team made sure I always understood the reason behind every task. I never felt like an outsider — the team was genuinely welcoming. I left this program feeling genuinely prepared for the industry.

I came here to learn a skill and left having learned almost nothing. Team meetings excluded interns entirely, leaving us in the dark. I was repeatedly given contradictory instructions from different supervisors. The internship added nothing to my resume and everything to my stress levels.

Honestly, this exceeded every expectation I had going in. My ideas were taken seriously in team meetings from the start. I had weekly one-on-ones with my mentor which were truly valuable. This internship made my resume stronger and my mindset sharper.

I was treated as free labor rather than a future professional. Colleagues were dismissive and some were outright rude to interns. The work assigned had no relevance to my field or the skills I wanted to build. I sincerely hope the company restructures how it handles interns.

The internship started poorly and unfortunately only got worse. Team meetings excluded interns entirely, leaving us in the dark. I was repeatedly given contradictory instructions from different supervisors. It was a discouraging start to what I hoped would be a great career.

My confidence as a professional grew significantly during these months. My ideas were taken seriously in team meetings from the start. I never felt like an outsider — the team was genuinely welcoming. The experience was transformational and I am grateful for every moment.

This internship completely changed how I think about the industry. I worked on a dashboard that is now used company-wide — very fulfilling. I attended client calls and strategy sessions that gave me real exposure. This is the gold standard of what an internship should look like.

This program is clearly designed with the intern development in mind. The projects were meaningful and directly tied to real business outcomes. The company invests in interns as if they are long-term hires. I could not have asked for a better environment to start my career.

I never expected an internship to teach me this much in such a short time. My manager connected me with senior leaders who gave me incredible advice. I built a portfolio piece I am genuinely proud to show employers. The experience was transformational and I am grateful for every moment.

The toxic culture here made it hard to even show up each day. I worked overtime regularly but my extra effort was never acknowledged. Team meetings excluded interns entirely, leaving us in the dark. I caution any student considering this company to look elsewhere first.

No one prepared me for how disorganized this place actually is. Colleagues were dismissive and some were outright rude to interns. I had to ask for feedback four times before receiving any response. I am glad it is over and I have no intention of returning.

I raised multiple concerns and was consistently dismissed or ignored. The onboarding process was nonexistent — I had to figure everything out alone. Team meetings excluded interns entirely, leaving us in the dark. I left with no portfolio piece, no reference, and no useful skill.

This internship completely changed how I think about the industry. The work-life balance was better than I expected for an internship. The team made sure I always understood the reason behind every task. This is the gold standard of what an internship should look like.

From day one I felt like a real team member, not just an intern. The technical mentorship I received here is something I will carry for years. The team made sure I always understood the reason behind every task. Without doubt, the best professional decision I made this year.

I raised multiple concerns and was consistently dismissed or ignored. The internship had no defined goals, milestones, or learning outcomes. The onboarding process was nonexistent — I had to figure everything out alone. This experience set my professional development back rather than forward.

The experience was deeply frustrating and professionally unfulfilling. The onboarding process was nonexistent — I had to figure everything out alone. My requests for career guidance were brushed off or postponed indefinitely. I would not recommend this program to anyone who values their time.

I came in as a student and left feeling like a professional. I built a portfolio piece I am genuinely proud to show employers. I was given ownership of a feature that shipped to real users. If I could do it again, I would without a single hesitation.
